blocks.body: Past Perfect vs Past Perfect Continuous — Past Perfect = had + V3 dla rezultatu lub faktu zakończonego przed innym momentem w przeszłości, a Past Perfect Continuous = had been + V-ing dla procesu albo czasu trwania aż do tego momentu.

{"level":"B1","intro":"Past Perfect zwykle odpowiada na pytanie what had happened albo what was already true by then. Past Perfect Continuous zwykle odpowiada na pytanie what had been going on before then albo how long coś trwało do tamtego momentu.","rules":[{"label":"PAST PERFECT","color":"blue","text":"had + V3 dla zakończonej czynności, rezultatu albo faktu, który był już gotowy przed innym momentem w przeszłości.","example":"She had left before I arrived. / By the time the bus came, they had already found seats."},{"label":"PAST PERFECT CONTINUOUS","color":"emerald","text":"had been + V-ing dla procesu albo czasu trwania, które prowadziły do innego wydarzenia lub momentu w przeszłości.","example":"They had been waiting for an hour before the bus came. / He was tired because he had been working all day."},{"label":"KEY QUESTION","color":"violet","text":"Zadaj sobie pytanie: what had happened / what was already true by then? czy what had been going on / how long before then?","example":"gotowy rezultat przed tym momentem / proces albo długość trwania do tego momentu"}]}

{"title":"1. Szybkie porównanie","intro":"Oba czasy patrzą wstecz od innego momentu w przeszłości, ale punkt ciężkości jest inny:","rows":[{"en":"Past Perfect","ua":"zakończony rezultat albo fakt przed momentem w przeszłości","note":"She had locked the door before she left."},{"en":"Past Perfect Continuous","ua":"proces albo długość trwania przed momentem w przeszłości","note":"She had been cleaning for an hour before she left."},{"en":"before / after","ua":"jedna czynność zakończyła się wcześniej niż inna czynność w przeszłości","note":"After we had eaten, we went out."},{"en":"by the time","ua":"gotowy rezultat istniał już przed innym wydarzeniem w przeszłości","note":"By the time I arrived, they had finished."},{"en":"for / since / how long","ua":"czas trwania albo aktywność aż do tamtego momentu","note":"How long had you been waiting?"},{"en":"until then / before that","ua":"proces albo okres trwający do tamtego momentu","note":"Until then, he had been living with his aunt."},{"en":"already / just / never","ua":"często wskazują na zakończony rezultat, doświadczenie albo fakt sprzed tamtego momentu","note":"She had already left. / He had never seen snow before that trip."},{"en":"result before then vs activity until then","ua":"had + V3 vs had been + V-ing","note":"I had repaired the bike. / I had been repairing the bike all morning."}],"warning":"Jeśli rezultat był już gotowy przed tamtym momentem w przeszłości, wybierz Past Perfect. Jeśli ważna jest aktywność albo czas trwania prowadzące do tego momentu, wybierz Past Perfect Continuous."}

{"title":"2. Formy obu czasów","intro":"Porównaj podstawowe wzory twierdzące, przeczące i pytające.","items":[{"label":"Past Perfect","title":"Twierdzenie","subtitle":"Subject + had + V3: I had finished."},{"label":"Past Perfect","title":"Przeczenie","subtitle":"Subject + hadn't + V3: She hadn't finished before lunch."},{"label":"Past Perfect","title":"Pytanie","subtitle":"Had + subject + V3? Had she already left before you called?"},{"label":"Past Perfect Continuous","title":"Twierdzenie","subtitle":"Subject + had been + V-ing: I had been studying."},{"label":"Past Perfect Continuous","title":"Przeczenie","subtitle":"Subject + hadn't been + V-ing: They hadn't been sleeping well before the trip."},{"label":"Past Perfect Continuous","title":"Pytanie","subtitle":"Had + subject + been + V-ing? How long had you been waiting?"}]}

{"title":"3. Co decyduje o wyborze czasu","sections":[{"label":"Gotowy rezultat przed tamtym momentem","color":"blue","description":"Wybierz Past Perfect, gdy czynność była już zakończona i ważny jest gotowy rezultat, fakt albo kolejność wydarzeń. before, after, by the time, already, just i never często prowadzą na ten wybór.","examples":[{"en":"By the time we reached the station, the train had already left.","ua":"Rezultat był już prawdą przed tamtym momentem."},{"en":"After he had locked the shop, he went home.","ua":"Najpierw zakończona czynność, potem druga czynność w przeszłości."}]},{"label":"Proces lub czas trwania do tamtego momentu","color":"emerald","description":"Wybierz Past Perfect Continuous, gdy ważny jest proces albo długość trwania. Często pojawiają się tu for, since, how long, until then i before that.","examples":[{"en":"They had been waiting for forty minutes before the doctor called them in.","ua":"Najważniejsza jest długość trwania przed innym wydarzeniem w przeszłości."},{"en":"How long had she been studying before the exam started?","ua":"Pytanie dotyczy bezpośrednio długości trwania przed tym momentem."}]},{"label":"Ta sama sytuacja, inny punkt ciężkości","color":"amber","description":"Sytuacja może być podobna, ale znaczenie się zmienia: gotowy rezultat przed tamtym momentem albo proces trwający do niego.","examples":[{"en":"I had written the report before the meeting.","ua":"Raport był już gotowy przed spotkaniem."},{"en":"I had been writing the report until the meeting started.","ua":"Najważniejszy jest proces trwający do spotkania."}],"note":"Past Perfect często odpowiada na What had happened?, a Past Perfect Continuous częściej odpowiada na What had been going on? albo How long?"},{"label":"Przyczyna widocznego skutku w przeszłości","color":"violet","description":"Wybierz Past Perfect Continuous, gdy wcześniejszy proces wyjaśnia stan albo rezultat w przeszłości.","examples":[{"en":"He was tired because he had been working since dawn.","ua":"Zmęczenie w przeszłości jest wyjaśnione wcześniejszym procesem."},{"en":"The ground was wet because it had been raining.","ua":"Rezultat w przeszłości ma swoją przyczynę we wcześniejszej aktywności."}]}]}

{"title":"4. Typowe błędy","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"blue","title":"Wybieranie Past Perfect, gdy główną ideą jest proces albo długość trwania","wrong":"❌ She had cleaned the flat since 6 o'clock before the guests arrived.","right":"✅ She had been cleaning the flat since 6 o'clock before the guests arrived."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"emerald","title":"Mieszanie formy Continuous z gotowym rezultatem","wrong":"❌ I had been finished the report before lunch.","right":"✅ I had finished the report before lunch."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"violet","title":"Używanie formy Continuous z czasownikami stanu","wrong":"❌ He had been knowing her for years before that.","right":"✅ He had known her for years before that."},{"label":"Mistake 4","color":"amber","title":"Używanie continuous dla gotowej liczby albo podsumowania","wrong":"❌ By the time I checked, she had been writing five emails.","right":"✅ By the time I checked, she had written five emails."}]}

{"title":"5. Czasowniki stanu i ważna uwaga","items":["know, like, believe, understand, want i need zwykle występują w Past Perfect, a nie w Past Perfect Continuous.","Powiedz I had known him for years before that, a nie I had been knowing him for years before that.","for i since same w sobie nie oznaczają Continuous: przy stanach często wybieramy Past Perfect, na przykład They had known each other since school.","Najprostsza zasada jest taka: stan = Past Perfect, czynność / proces = Past Perfect Continuous."]}

{"title":"6. Krótki algorytm wyboru","items":["Czy liczy się gotowy rezultat, zakończona czynność albo fakt, który był już prawdziwy przed tamtym momentem? → wybierz Past Perfect.","Czy masz before, after, by the time, already, just albo never z naciskiem na to, co wydarzyło się wcześniej? → zwykle wybierz Past Perfect.","Czy pytasz how long? albo masz for / since, until then lub before that z naciskiem na czas trwania? → wybierz Past Perfect Continuous.","Czy chcesz pokazać, że stan albo rezultat w przeszłości wynikał z wcześniejszego procesu? → wybierz Past Perfect Continuous.","Czy czasownik oznacza stan? → zostań przy Past Perfect, nawet jeśli okres trwał do tamtego momentu."]}

{"title":"7. Strony i powiązane tematy tego bloku","items":[{"label":"Tenses","url":"/pl/theory/tenses","current":false},{"label":"Past Perfect","url":"/pl/theory/past-perfect","current":false},{"label":"Past Perfect Continuous","url":"/pl/theory/past-perfect-continuous","current":false},{"label":"Past Perfect vs Past Perfect Continuous","url":"/pl/theory/tenses/past-perfect-vs-past-perfect-continuous","current":true},{"label":"Present Perfect","url":"/pl/theory/present-perfect","current":false},{"label":"Present Perfect Continuous","url":"/pl/theory/present-perfect-continuous","current":false},{"label":"Present Perfect vs Present Perfect Continuous","url":"/pl/theory/tenses/present-perfect-vs-present-perfect-continuous","current":false}]}
